title: SpeedTrader Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)

meta_description: SpeedTrader Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). SpeedTrader (4.1★, Free). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tips. Updated 2026.
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using SpeedTrader — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
What is SpeedTrader?

SpeedTrader is a direct market access (DMA) broker designed exclusively for active day traders and scalpers who execute 50+ trades daily. Founded in 1999 and now part of Mint Global Markets, SpeedTrader routes your orders directly to exchanges—ARCA, NASDAQ, NYSE, BATS/EDGX, and ECNs—rather than through market makers, giving you sub-millisecond execution speeds that matter when your edge depends on timing. Unlike retail brokers that generate revenue by selling your order flow, SpeedTrader eliminates payment for order flow entirely, meaning your orders hit the market on your terms. With a 4.1/5 rating and no monthly platform fees, SpeedTrader appeals to professional traders, prop firm affiliates, and serious short sellers who need reliable locate services and routing flexibility that conventional brokers simply don't offer.

How to Create Your SpeedTrader Account

Step 1: Visit the SpeedTrader Website and Start the Application

Go to the SpeedTrader website and click the account opening link. You'll be asked to select your account type: individual day trader, joint account, or corporate/prop firm. Choose the category that matches your trading structure, as this determines compliance requirements and account features available to you.

Step 2: Provide Personal and Financial Information

Complete the account application form with your legal name, address, Social Security number, date of birth, and employment status. SpeedTrader will also ask about your trading experience, annual income, net worth, and investment objectives. Be honest here—they're assessing whether you meet Pattern Day Trader (PDT) requirements and whether you understand the risks of DMA execution. You'll need to confirm that you have at least $25,000 in liquid capital to fund your account, which is a hard regulatory requirement.

Step 3: Complete Identity Verification

SpeedTrader uses third-party identity verification. You'll need to provide a government-issued ID (driver's license or passport) and may be asked for a utility bill or recent bank statement to verify your address. The process is typically electronic and takes 5-10 minutes. If you're applying for a margin account, SpeedTrader will conduct an enhanced review of your trading history and financial situation.

Step 4: Fund Your Account

Once approved, you'll receive instructions to wire $25,000 minimum to SpeedTrader's designated account. SpeedTrader accepts wire transfers and ACH deposits. Wire transfers typically clear same-day or next-day; ACH takes 3-5 business days. Your account will be restricted from trading until the full minimum is deposited and cleared.

Step 5: Download and Configure Your Trading Platform

SpeedTrader offers three platform options: SpeedTrader PRO (their proprietary platform), Sterling Trader Pro, or DAS Trader Pro. Download the platform corresponding to your choice, log in with your new credentials, and proceed to the first-time setup section below. Most traders complete the entire account opening process—from application to funded, platform-ready account—in 2-3 business days, provided your wire transfer clears quickly.

Setting Up SpeedTrader for the First Time

Launch Your Platform and Review the Dashboard Layout

When you open SpeedTrader PRO or your chosen platform, you'll see the workspace broken into panels: a live ticker on the left, your open positions and P&L in the center, order entry controls on the right, and Level 2 data showing real-time bid-ask depth for your selected stock. Customize this layout by dragging panels to fit your workflow—day traders often want Level 2 maximized and positions minimized until end-of-day review.

Configure Your Routing Preferences

SpeedTrader's primary advantage is multiple exchange routing. In Settings, designate your default routing: ARCA for midpoint execution, NASDAQ for tech stocks with high volume, NYSE for large-cap liquidity, or ECNs like EDGX for cost control. You can also set conditional routing—for example, "route orders over 10,000 shares to ARCA, under 10,000 to NASDAQ." Get this right early, because routing is where your execution edge lives. If you don't specify, SpeedTrader defaults to market-maker-friendly routing, which defeats the purpose of using a DMA broker.

Set Up Alerts and Risk Management Rules

Under Alerts, enable price-level notifications for your watchlist, so you're alerted when a stock hits your predetermined entry or exit level. More critically, configure your daily loss limit and max position size. If you're funded with $25,000 and trade with 4:1 margin, set a hard stop-loss alert at $2,500 loss (10% daily max)—SpeedTrader will flag you visually and can auto-flatten positions if you've enabled that feature. Risk management rules prevent the catastrophic $5,000-in-five-minutes blowout that kills small accounts.

Connect Your Data Feed and Charting Tool

SpeedTrader's platform includes Level 2 data and real-time quotes, but the charting is execution-focused, not analysis-focused. Integrate a separate charting tool—most traders link ThinkorSwim, Thinkorswim, or Bloomberg Terminal for technical analysis—then use SpeedTrader's order entry for execution. This split-tool approach is standard among day traders: one tool for setup and bias, one tool for execution and speed.

Essential Features You Should Know

1. Paper Trading Mode

Before you risk real capital, use SpeedTrader's paper trading environment to simulate live execution. You'll get real-time quotes and Level 2 data, but your trades execute in a virtual account. This is where you test routing preferences, order types, and your reaction time to fast-moving stocks. Spend at least 20 hours in paper trading—you'll discover that your technical setup might be solid but your execution discipline needs work.

2. Multiple Platform Options: Sterling Trader Pro, DAS Trader Pro, and SpeedTrader PRO

SpeedTrader doesn't lock you into a single interface. Sterling Trader Pro is minimalist and keyboard-focused for scalpers. DAS Trader Pro is feature-rich with advanced charting. SpeedTrader PRO (proprietary) is modern and cloud-based. Test all three in paper trading—the right platform reduces decision lag by microseconds. Many traders switch platforms every 6 months as their strategy evolves.

3. Level 2 Market Data and Depth Visualization

Level 2 shows you the exact bid-ask spread, order sizes at each price level, and which market makers are on each side. This is how scalpers find hidden liquidity and identify when a breakout is real versus trapped retail traders. Learn to read Level 2: if you see 50,000 shares stacked at the ask with only 100 shares at the bid, that's a setup. If the ask suddenly clears, that's your scalp trigger. Most retail platforms hide this from you; SpeedTrader puts it front-and-center.

4. Short Locate Services and Borrow Availability

SpeedTrader has relationships with multiple prime brokers and maintains a robust short-locate library. If you want to short a hard-to-borrow stock like a low-float runner or a recent IPO, SpeedTrader's locate team can often source shares within minutes. Without a proper locate, your short order gets rejected, killing your trade. This is why short sellers specifically choose SpeedTrader—your broker's locate service is literally the difference between executing your edge and missing it.

5. Risk Management and Position Alerts

Configure position-level stop-losses, profit-targets, and max drawdown alerts. You can set an alert that pops up the moment you hit 3R profit on a trade, reminding you to take profits. You can also set hard brackets—buy 1,000 shares with an automatic 200-share profit-take at +$0.30 and a 300-share stop-loss at -$0.50. These aren't just nice-to-haves; they're survival mechanics that prevent emotional decisions in fast markets.

6. Education and Trading Community Access

SpeedTrader includes access to webinars and trading tutorials focused on DMA concepts, routing strategy, and scalping mechanics. These aren't generic "investing 101" courses—they're taught by professional day traders and cover real topics like level 2 reading, momentum scanning, and pre-market setup analysis. Join the SpeedTrader community forums to see how other active traders are using routing and risk rules.

7. Mobile App for Monitoring Positions

SpeedTrader's mobile app lets you monitor open positions, close trades, and receive alerts while away from your desk. However, the mobile app is monitoring-only—order entry is limited. Use it to check on your midday P&L or flatten a position if you get stopped out by market news, but day trading from a phone is a recipe for expensive mistakes.

SpeedTrader Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

SpeedTrader's pricing structure is straightforward: no monthly platform fees. Instead, you pay per-share and per-trade commissions, plus ECN fees depending on which exchanges you route through.

Typical Commission Breakdown:

  • Per-Share Commission: $0.001 to $0.003 per share (usually depends on your monthly trade volume and account size).
  • Per-Trade Minimum: $0-$3 per trade, depending on your tier.
  • ECN Fees: $0 to $0.003 per share when you add liquidity to ARCA, EDGX, or other ECNs (sometimes rebated if you remove liquidity).
  • Platform Fees: $0/month for SpeedTrader PRO or Sterling Trader Pro; DAS Trader Pro may have a small monthly fee ($50-$100, but often waived for frequent traders).

For Active Scalpers (50-200 trades/day):

Expect to pay $50-$150/month in total commissions if you're scalping $500K-$1M in daily volume. Your per-share costs drop as you volume tier up, so this tier is most profitable for traders with an edge that can sustain consistent 50+ trade days. Scalpers should use ARCA or EDGX routing to minimize ECN fees.

For Position Day Traders (5-20 trades/day):

Monthly costs typically run $10-$50, since you're taking fewer but larger-position trades. Your commission burn is lower, so you need a bigger per-trade edge. This tier works for traders trading the first 30 minutes of market open or momentum runners with $2K-$5K risk per trade.

For Prop Firm Affiliates and Funded Traders:

Many prop firms (Apex Group, TopStep, others) negotiate bulk rates with SpeedTrader, so your per-share commission drops to $0.0005-$0.001 and minimums vanish. If you're prop-firm backed, ask your firm's administrator about their SpeedTrader pricing agreement—it's often 30-50% cheaper than retail rates.

The Bottom Line on Pricing: SpeedTrader's no-platform-fee model is a win compared to Interactive Brokers ($10/month) or Tastyworks ($0 but limited routing). You only pay for actual execution, not access. For traders doing 5-20 trades/day, you'll spend less than $100/month—far cheaper than paying PFOF kickbacks to a retail broker selling your order flow.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of SpeedTrader

1. Master Your Routing Strategy Before You Trade Live

Not all orders fill equally on all exchanges. ARCA fills midpoint trades instantly but has wider spreads on illiquid stocks. NASDAQ is best for high-volume tech runners. NYSE is best for large-cap spreads. Spend time in paper trading testing which routing works best for your setup. Once you find your edge, lock it in via conditional routing rules so you're not making decisions in real-time.

2. Use Hotkeys to Reduce Execution Lag

SpeedTrader and Sterling Trader Pro are keyboard-optimized platforms. Customize hotkeys: set one key to "buy 1000 @ market ARCA," another to "sell half position," another to "flatten all." Shaving 500 milliseconds off your order entry can be the difference between filling at your target and getting run over on a scalp. Pro traders memorize 20+ hotkeys.

3. Monitor and Adjust Your ECN Fee Impact Daily

ECN fees add up fast if you're routing poorly. Check your daily fill report every night: Which exchanges filled your orders? How much did you pay in ECN fees? If you're bleeding $200/week on unnecessary ECN fees, adjust your routing rules. Sometimes paying $0.003/share to remove liquidity is worth it; sometimes it's not. Let your fill data tell you.

4. Locates Expire—Renew Hard-to-Borrow Shorts Daily

If you're holding a short position in a hard-to-borrow stock, your locate expires at end-of-day. Call SpeedTrader's locate desk first thing the next morning to renew before market open, or you'll get bought in during the first 15 minutes of trading. Set a calendar reminder for 9:25 AM EST if you're planning a multi-day short.

5. Track Your Volume Tier and Negotiate Commission Reductions

SpeedTrader's per-share rates drop as you volume tier up. If you're at $10M monthly volume, you should be paying $0.001/share, not $0.003. Once a quarter, call your SpeedTrader rep and review your volume—request a tier bump if you've qualified. Most traders leave money on the table by not asking.

6. Use Paper Trading to Test Platform Changes Before Live Trading

Whenever you change your routing setup, test it in paper trading for at least 100 fills before going live. A small tweak to your DAS Trader Pro hotkey configuration or a change to your conditional routing rules can have unintended consequences. Paper trading reveals these bugs before they cost you real money.

7. Join the SpeedTrader Community and Learn from Other Traders

SpeedTrader's forums and Discord communities include prop firm traders, scalpers, and short specialists. Ask specific questions about routing (e.g., "anyone else seeing wide NASDAQ spreads on low-float runners today?"). The collective knowledge is invaluable, and many traders openly share their setups and commission structures.

Common SpeedTrader Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue 1: Your Order Gets Rejected Mid-Trade or You Get "Shares Not Available" Errors

Usually means your routing or short locate is invalid. Check: (1) Is your short locate still active? Call the locate desk to verify. (2) Is your routing preference set to an exchange that's open? Avoid routing to closed exchanges. (3) Are you using a valid order type for your chosen exchange? Not all exchanges accept all order types. Restart your platform, verify your routing rule, and try again. If you're consistently getting rejected, call SpeedTrader's execution desk—there may be a configuration issue with your account.

Issue 2: Your Fills Are Much Worse Than Your Expected Price

This usually indicates poor routing or you're trading illiquid stocks. Check your fill report: (1) Which exchange filled your order? If it was a bad-fill exchange, change your routing. (2) What time of day? If you're trading 3:50 PM when spreads widen, your fills will be worse. (3) What size? Large orders on illiquid stocks will move the market against you—split your order or use limit orders instead of market orders. SpeedTrader execution is excellent, but a 10,000-share market order on a sub-$5 penny stock will always fill poorly.

Issue 3: Platform Crashes During Market Open

Rare, but it happens. Always have a backup: (1) Immediately log into your backup SpeedTrader platform option (if you were using Sterling Trader, log into DAS Trader Pro). (2) Check the market—if you have open positions, close them immediately via the backup platform. (3) Call SpeedTrader's support line. They can flatten your positions remotely if both platforms are down. Pro traders keep their phone number pinned to their trading desk: (800) 562-8694.

Issue 4: You're Getting Charged Unexpected Fees or Commission Rates Changed

Review your latest statement and call your account rep to clarify. Sometimes platform fees appear if you switched to DAS Trader Pro without negotiating a waiver. Sometimes an ECN fee increase happens when your volume drops a tier. Get clarification in writing. SpeedTrader's billing is usually transparent, but traders miss updates if they don't monitor statements monthly.

Is SpeedTrader Worth It? Our Verdict

SpeedTrader earns a 4.1/5 rating and deserves it—this is professional-grade DMA execution at competitive commissions with zero platform fees. If you're executing 50+ trades daily as a scalper or prop firm trader, the routing control and no-PFOF guarantee are invaluable; your execution edge depends on millisecond advantages and locate reliability, and SpeedTrader delivers both. If you're a position trader doing 5-20 trades per day, you'll pay $20-$50/month in commissions—far less than retail brokers charging $10/month plus PFOF, and you get superior routing and short services to boot.

Skip SpeedTrader if you have less than $25,000 to trade with, don't understand Level 2 data or routing, or are looking for a simple, beginner-friendly interface. SpeedTrader assumes you know what DMA means and why it matters. If you're comparing, Interactive Brokers offers DMA at similar prices but with more asset classes (forex, futures, crypto), while Centerpoint Securities specializes in short locates with slightly more aggressive pricing. For pure equities day trading, SpeedTrader is the standard among professionals—your commission savings and execution quality will compound over thousands of trades.

See our full SpeedTrader review for performance benchmarks and trader testimonials, or check our SpeedTrader vs. Centerpoint comparison if short-selling locates are your primary concern.
